questions.question: Define percentage and explain why it is called 'per hundred'.
questions.answer: Percentage means per hundred. It represents a fraction with denominator 100. For example, 25% means 25 out of 100.

questions.question: Explain how fractions can be converted into percentages.
questions.answer: To convert a fraction into percentage, multiply the fraction by 100. Example: 3/4 × 100 = 75%.

questions.question: Explain how percentages can be written as fractions.
questions.answer: A percentage can be written as a fraction with denominator 100. Example: 24% = 24/100 = 6/25.

questions.question: Explain how to find the percentage of a quantity.
questions.answer: To find y% of a number, multiply the number by y/100. Example: 25% of 120 = 25/100 × 120 = 30.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between fractions, decimals, and percentages.
questions.answer: Fractions, decimals, and percentages represent the same value in different forms. Example: 1/2 = 0.5 = 50%.

questions.question: Explain why percentages are useful for comparing quantities.
questions.answer: Percentages standardize quantities to a base of 100, making comparisons easier even when totals are different.

questions.question: Explain percentage increase with an example.
questions.answer: Percentage increase measures how much a value has increased compared to the original value. Example: Price increased from ₹30 to ₹42. Increase = ₹12. Percentage increase = (12/30) × 100 = 40%.

questions.question: Explain percentage decrease with an example.
questions.answer: Percentage decrease measures how much a value has decreased compared to the original value.

questions.question: Explain the concept of profit.
questions.answer: Profit occurs when the selling price of an item is greater than its cost price.

questions.question: Explain the concept of loss.
questions.answer: Loss occurs when the selling price of an item is less than its cost price.

questions.question: Explain how profit percentage is calculated.
questions.answer: Profit percentage = (Profit / Cost Price) × 100.

questions.question: Explain how loss percentage is calculated.
questions.answer: Loss percentage = (Loss / Cost Price) × 100.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of discount in shopping.
questions.answer: Discount is the reduction in the marked price of an item offered to customers.

questions.question: Explain the concept of marked price and selling price.
questions.answer: Marked price is the price listed on an item, while selling price is the actual price paid after discount.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of tax with an example.
questions.answer: Tax is a percentage amount added to the price of goods or services and paid to the government.

questions.question: Explain the concept of interest in banking.
questions.answer: Interest is the extra money paid by banks when money is deposited or charged when money is borrowed.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of principal and rate of interest.
questions.answer: Principal is the original amount deposited or borrowed. Rate of interest is the percentage charged or paid each year.

questions.question: Explain simple interest with an example.
questions.answer: Simple interest is calculated only on the original principal amount.

questions.question: Explain the concept of compounding.
questions.answer: Compounding occurs when interest earned is added to the principal so that future interest is calculated on the increased amount.

questions.question: Explain why percentages greater than 100 can exist.
questions.answer: Percentages greater than 100 indicate that a value has exceeded the original quantity.

questions.question: Explain how ratios can be converted into percentages.
questions.answer: Convert the ratio into a fraction and multiply by 100 to get the percentage.

questions.question: Explain how bar models help in understanding percentages.
questions.answer: Bar models visually represent proportions and help students understand percentage relationships easily.

questions.question: Explain why estimation is important in percentage calculations.
questions.answer: Estimation helps verify answers quickly and improves number sense.

questions.question: Explain how percentages are used in real life.
questions.answer: Percentages are used in exams, shopping discounts, taxes, population growth, and financial calculations.

questions.question: Explain the overall classification of percentage applications in mathematics.
questions.answer: Percentages are used in comparisons, growth and decrease calculations, profit and loss analysis, and financial computations such as interest.
